news.headline: DNO ASA completes USD 600M bond placement with 8.5% coupon rate, met strong demand, oversubscribed.
news.summary: On 14th March 2025, Norwegian oil and gas operator, DNO ASA, successfully concluded a private placement of USD 600 million in fresh five-year senior unsecured bonds, carrying an 8.5 percent coupon rate. The bond offering received substantial interest from investors in US, Nordic, and global markets, surpassing the anticipated demand and resulting in oversubscription.

news.headline: Putin requests US envoy to deliver messages to Trump after discussions.
DEMIRE AG making preliminary announcement for financial reports under WpHG [German Securities Act - Articles 114, 115, 117].
news.summary: DEIMIRE German Midcap Real Estate AG has released a preliminary announcement regarding the publication of financial reports as required by Articles 114, 115, and 117 of the German Securities Act (WpHG). This disclosure is necessary for transparency in the financial sector and to comply with regulatory requirements. The company's financial reports contain vital information about its performance and position in the market. Such reports are crucial for investors, analysts, and stakeholders to analyze the company's financial health and make informed decisions. This announcement indicates the company's commitment to following regulatory standards and keeping the public informed about its financial status.

news.headline: Inventor from Md. creates A Z CUP, a modified drinking cup with built-in straw, aiming to reduce waste.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on March 14, 2025, an inventor from Silver Spring, Maryland shared their inspiration behind creating the A Z CUP. The inventor believed that there needed to be an easier way to access a straw while drinking from a cup. This new cup design is aimed at simplifying the process and reducing waste associated with using individual straws. The innovation seeks to provide a convenient solution for consumers while also promoting sustainability.

news.headline: Miracle-Gro partners with "Disney's Snow White." Collaboration aims to promote gardening joy. News released on March 14, 2025.
news.summary: A diverse partnership is encouraging individuals of various ages to partake in the delight of gardening. On March 14, 2025, in MARYSVILLE, Ohio, Miracle-Gro, a prominent gardening product brand, revealed its collaboration with "Disney's Snow White," a modern take on the classic fairy tale set to music, showcasing the importance of connecting with nature. This joint effort aims to engage a wider audience and promote the benefits of gardening.

news.headline: FDA campaign "The Real Cost" prevented 450,000 youths from starting e-cigarette use; ads reduced use to lowest in a decade.
news.summary: A recent study co-authored by scientists from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration revealed that the agency's youth e-cigarette prevention campaign, "The Real Cost," has led to a significant decrease in youth e-cigarette use. The campaign, which includes advertisements, has successfully driven youth e-cigarette use to its lowest level in the past ten years. The study was released in Silver Spring, Md., on March 14, 2025. This successful initiative resulted in a substantial reduction in the number of young individuals using e-cigarettes, marking a positive step in combating youth e-cigarette consumption.

news.headline: Putin sends message to Trump via US envoy during truce talks.
Managers and close associates disclose transactions: [Number] made public. Compliance with transparency rules.
US consumer confidence hits 4-month low as inflation fears soar, with UMich reporting a sharp drop to 57.9 in March.
news.summary: In the U.S., consumer confidence hit a four-month low, with data from the University of Michigan showing a sharp decline. Inflation expectations reached their highest level in decades, leading to concerns about a possible stagflationary environment. The March Consumer Sentiment Index dropped to 57.9 from February's 64.7, falling 11% monthly and 22% from December 2024. Consumer expectations for future economic conditions also fell sharply, with the Consumer Expectations Index dropping to 54.2 from 64.0. The Current Conditions Index slightly decreased from 65.7 to 63.5. Year-ahead inflation expectations rose from 4.3% in February to 4.9% in March, the highest since November 2022.

news.headline: Russia is using Bitcoin and Ethereum to evade sanctions, with oil deals worth tens of millions monthly with China.
news.summary: Russia has begun utilizing cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) for a portion of its oil transactions with China and India, in an effort to navigate around Western sanctions. These transactions involve converting local currencies such as yuan and rupees into rubles through intermediaries, with stablecoins like USDT also being used. However, there is a risk as USDT's issuer, Tether (USDT), can freeze funds under regulatory scrutiny. An insider from a Russian oil company revealed that monthly crypto-based sales to China amount to several tens of millions of dollars. This shift in strategy commenced in December following Russia's legal adjustments allowing for cryptocurrency use.

news.headline: NAMI issues statement on potential 50% staff cut at SAMHSA, reports suggest.
news.summary: Today, the National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) issued a statement regarding reports on potential staff cuts at the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Agency (SAMHSA). The reduction could be as high as 50% according to recent news.

news.headline: Chicken Salad Chick opens new location in Pflugerville, TX on March 14, 2025. Owned by Meauxmentum Strategies & Investments, LLC.
news.summary: In PFLUGERVILLE, Texas, on March 14, 2025, Meauxmentum Strategies & Investments, LLC (MSI), a top restaurant development and shared services management firm, revealed the opening of a new Chicken Salad Chick outlet.

news.headline: AGM to elect new board members in 2025. Item 6: Election of new directors.
Inventor from Lorton, Va creates M Y I A S DRIVE speed monitoring system to help parents track teenage drivers' behavior.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on March 14, 2025, an inventor from Lorton, Virginia, announced the creation of a new product called the M Y I A S DRIVE. The inventor stated that the motivation behind the invention was to offer parents a tool to track their teenage drivers' behavior behind the wheel. The device serves as a way for parents to monitor their children's driving habits when they are not present. The M Y I A S DRIVE automatically provides updates on the teenagers' driving skills and behaviors, aiming to enhance safety on the roads.

news.headline: ORDNX wins LSEG Lipper Fund Awards USA 2025 for strong risk-adjusted performance over 3-, 5-, and 10-year periods.
news.summary: A fund known as ORDNX has recently been recognized as a winner at the LSEG Lipper Fund Awards USA 2025. This particular fund, the North Square Preferred and Securities Income Fund, stood out for its impressive performance over the past three, five, and 10 years in comparison to similar funds in the category of Best Flexible Income. This recognition highlights ORDNX's strong risk-adjusted returns in the industry, solidifying its reputation as a top player in the market.

news.headline: WH Press Sec. Leavitt: There is nothing on Trump's schedule to talk with Putin today but Trump's schedule could change.

BMW is facing a $1 billion hit due to tariffs and supply issues, leading to an 8.4% revenue decline in 2024 to 142.38 billion euros. The carmaker's automotive revenue dropped by 5.6% to 124.92 billion euros while motorcycle revenue increased by 0.2% to 3.22 billion euros.
news.summary: Luxury car manufacturer BMW faced a 8.4% decrease in revenue for 2024, amounting to 142.38 billion euros. The company cited challenges amidst the trade tensions between the U.S. and EU, with President Donald Trump threatening increased tariffs on European car imports starting April 2. Specifically, automotive revenue experienced a 5.6% drop to 124.92 billion euros while motorcycle revenue saw a slight 0.2% increase to 3.22 billion euros. This information was released in a report recently published by the company. This story and more details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Vine Financial honored as ICBA ThinkTECH Accelerator Most Valuable Participant at ICBA LIVE, showcasing top fintech solutions.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas, Vine Financial was honored by the Independent Community Bankers of America® (ICBA) at the ICBA LIVE national convention in Nashville. This recognition was for being the Most Valuable Participant in the ThinkTECH Showcase. The prestigious MVP award, backed by TrueNorth, was given to Vine Financial for having the most advanced fintech offering that demonstrated exceptional program engagement and alignment with community bank business priorities. The event showcased various fintech solutions, showcasing innovations in the industry.

news.headline: Equity correction discussed in Bloomberg Surveillance show.
AirSculpt Technologies (NASDAQ:AIRS) stock rises with Q4 adjusted loss of 8 cents/share, sales $39.178M (down 17.7%), cases volume 3,064.
news.summary: AirSculpt Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AIRS) saw an increase in shares trading on Friday following their latest financial report. The company disclosed an adjusted loss per share of 8 cents, which was wider than the anticipated 4 cents loss by analysts. Quarterly sales stood at $39.178 million, reflecting a 17.7% decline from the previous year and falling short of the estimated $43.386 million. During the fourth quarter, case volume decreased by 16.7% to 3,064. Looking ahead, the company expects a challenging year-over-year comparison in the first quarter in terms of same-center revenue, but remains positive about future prospects.

news.headline: Sierra Metals to announce Q4 2024 & FY 2024 results on March 26, 2026 after market hours. Conference call on March 27, 2025.
news.summary: Sierra Metals Inc. (TSX: SMT | OTCQX: SMTSF | BVL: SMT) has announced that it intends to unveil its financial and operating performance for Q4 2024 and fiscal year 2024 on March 26, 2026, post-market closure. A conference call along with a webcast is scheduled for March 27, 2025, at 11:00 am (Eastern) to delve into these results. The webcast will be available in both English and Spanish versions. For those opting for the telephone option, the Canada/US toll-free number is 1-844-763-8274.

news.headline: Global second-hand products market sales expected to reach US$1.04 trillion by 2035 with 17.2% CAGR growth fueled by sustainability and affordability demands. Key players include ThredUp, eBay, and Carvana. Key drivers: consumer awareness of sustainability and economic considerations.
news.summary: The global second-hand products industry, valued at US$186.0 billion in 2024, is expected to reach US$1,044.0 billion by 2035 with an estimated CAGR of 17.2%. This growth is driven by consumer demand for sustainable and affordable options. Major players like ThredUp, eBay, and Carvana are dominating the market. Economic instability and inflation have also contributed to the rise of the second-hand market, especially in sectors like automobiles and technology. The industry is witnessing significant growth in online resale platforms and smart electronic devices. Key drivers include consumer awareness of sustainability and economic considerations. Recent developments include partnerships to expand resale services and the introduction of blockchain authentication for luxury resale.

news.headline: Congress repealed IRS rule on DeFi reporting. Questions remain on DeFi regulation. Bipartisan vote. Privacy concerns persist. Regulators seek balance.
news.summary: The DeFi industry is relieved after Congress voted to nullify a rule requiring reporting of crypto sales to the IRS. The rule issued in Dec 2024 would be effective in 2027 and was seen as burdensome. The bipartisan vote to repeal the rule indicates industry concern about privacy and regulation. Privacy concerns over user transactions were highlighted by industry experts, emphasizing the need for clear guidelines on blockchain-based privacy. While DeFi developers have worked with regulators on specific issues, a comprehensive regulatory framework is still lacking. Suggestions for regulation include zero-knowledge proofs to balance user privacy with compliance. The article also mentions a stablecoin bill approved by the Senate Banking Committee, indicating progress in crypto regulation. President Trump's pro-crypto measures and the establishment of a strategic Bitcoin reserve show a positive trend towards the industry. But the regulation for DeFi remains uncertain, with the need for a working group to study and recommend regulations for the 12 key areas related to DeFi.

news.headline: JAB revamps team to support strategic transformation, adds life insurance platform. Focus on growth with new leadership.
news.summary: Luxembourg-based investment firm JAB has recently restructured its team in order to facilitate its strategic evolution. Known for its success in pet insurance, the company is now expanding into global life insurance. This move aims to diversify JAB's portfolio and ensure sustainable long-term growth. The management team, led by Managing Partners Peter Harf, Joachim Creus, and Frank Engelen, is driving this transition. This strategic shift comes amidst the firm's continued dedication to consumer goods and services.

Crypto Fear and Greed Index 27/100 = Fear
Dow Jones surged 400 points after Chuck Schumer's decision; Nvidia and Tesla stocks reached highs.
news.summary: On Friday, the Dow Jones experienced an increase after Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer withdrew his opposition to a GOP spending bill. This led to a surge of 400 points in the Dow Jones. Additionally, companies like Nvidia and Tesla saw notable gains in their stock prices.

news.headline: Inventor from Royse City, Texas develops ROVER SAVER protective case for GPS rovers to prevent damage.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on March 14, 2025, a creator from Royse City, Texas, shared that they have significant expertise in land surveying and grew frustrated with the mishandling and damage of costly GPS rovers. As a solution, they developed the ROVER SAVER, a design featuring a cushioned protective shield for these devices.

news.headline: EU countries extend sanctions against Russia for six months - Statement.
Germany's SPD Leader Klingbeil: I am convinced SPD lawmakers will agree on Tuesday.
Ave Maria Bond Fund (NASDAQ: AVEFX) wins 2025 LSEG Lipper Awards for best A-rated corporate bond funds.
news.summary: Schwartz Investment Counsel, Inc., the investment adviser to the Ave Maria Mutual Funds, revealed that their Ave Maria Bond Fund (NASDAQ: AVEFX) has clinched the prestigious 2025 LSEG Lipper Fund Awards for the top A-rated corporate bond funds. This recognition was achieved for both the 3-year (among 10 funds) and 5-year periods (among 10 funds) concluding on November 30, 2024. Additionally, the Fund had also secured awards in 2024 for excelling as the best A-rated corporate bond funds for the 3-year (among 12 funds) period.

news.headline: J.P. Morgan Asset Management launches JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF (JUSA) on NYSE for consistent returns.
news.summary: J.P. Morgan Asset Management has introduced the JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF (JUSA) on the New York Stock Exchange on March 14, 2025. This fund is designed to offer investors exposure to U.S. equity while aiming for consistent returns. The launch aims to provide a new investment option for individuals looking to navigate the stock market. The ETF is geared towards delivering reliable performance in the large-cap sector, catering to those seeking growth opportunities and stability in their portfolios. Investors can now access this innovative ETF that combines research-driven strategies with the potential for growth in the U.S. equity market.

news.headline: Charles Schwab Corp's stock rises as core net new assets surge by 44% year-over-year to $48.0 billion in February 2025. Client assets are at $10.28 trillion, up 16% from February 2024.
news.summary: Shares of Charles Schwab Corp (NYSE:SCHW) saw an increase in trading on Friday following the release of their February 2025 activity report. The company reported that core net new assets from both new and existing clients totaled $48.0 billion, showing a significant 44% increase compared to the previous year. At the end of February, total client assets reached $10.28 trillion, marking a 16% rise from February 2024 but a slight 1% decrease from January 2025. This data reflects the company's continued growth and stability in the financial market.

news.headline: Ascot's private placement's first tranche closed.
news.summary: Last year, a total of 3,500 new cases of a rare health condition were reported in the country. This represents a 15% increase from the previous year. Additionally, the number of deaths related to this condition also rose by 10% to reach 500 fatalities. Experts attribute this rise to increased awareness and improved diagnostic techniques. Public health officials are working on expanding access to treatment for affected individuals to help reduce the mortality rate associated with this condition.

news.headline: NATO leader suggests improving ties with Russia after conflict, without specifying timeframe.
MDVIP opens 2 primary care practices in FL Gulf Coast - Dr. Ortega in Naples, Dr. Torres in Palm Harbor, offering personalized care.
news.summary: Naples-based physician Dr. Mailin Ortega, MD, and Palm Harbor-based physician Dr. Melissa Torres, DO, both board-certified, are providing patients with enhanced care through a personalized approach. Operating in Florida cities Naples and Palm Harbor, along with Boca Raton, these doctors emphasize spending more time and attention on each patient. Their dedication to offering individualized care sets them apart in the healthcare field. The services they offer are under MDVIP, a prominent company in the industry. This news was reported on March 14, 2025.

Trump requests Russia to spare surrounded Ukraine troops' lives amid escalating tensions. #Ukraine #Russia #Trump

WeRide's stock rises despite Q4 revenue dip. Q4 revenue: RMB140.8M ($19.3M), product revenue: RMB51.7M ($7.1M); Net loss: RMB245.5M ($33.6M).
news.summary: WeRide Inc. (NASDAQ: WRD) stocks saw an increase in trading on Friday following the release of their fourth-quarter financial report. The total revenue for the quarter was RMB140.8 million ($19.3 million), a slight decrease from RMB144.8 million reported in the same period in 2023. The company experienced a surge in fourth-quarter product revenue, reaching RMB51.7 million ($7.1 million), attributed to the strong sales performance of their robotaxis, robosweepers, and robovans. Conversely, service revenue dropped to RMB89.1 million ($12.2 million) due to decreased income from ADAS research and development services. WeRide reported a non-IFRS adjusted net loss of RMB245.5 million ($33.6 million) for the quarter, a significant increase from the RMB115.7 million loss reported in the prior-year quarter. The financial figures were as of December 31, 2024.

news.headline: Angeles Investments named 2025 Greenwich Best Investment Consultant for 8th year, managing $6.8B in assets and advising $31.5B.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, in Santa Monica, California, Angeles Investments, a diverse investment management company, was acknowledged by Coalition Greenwich as a top Investment Consultant for 2025. The firm manages around $6.8 billion in discretionary assets and oversees $31.5 billion in advisory assets.

Heartland Express, Inc. declares $0.02/share quarterly dividend, totaling $1.6M for 78.7M shares, marking 87th consecutive payout.
news.summary: Heartland Express, Inc. based in North Liberty, Iowa, declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.02 per share for shareholders of record on March 25, 2025. The dividend, totaling approximately $1.6 million, will be disbursed on April 4, 2025, to the Company's 78.7 million common stockholders. This dividend marks the eighty-seventh consecutive quarterly cash dividend issued by the company, bringing the total cash dividends paid to $556.8 million, which includes four special dividends distributed since the inception of the dividend program in the third quarter of 2003.

news.headline: LILYSILK launches exclusive Capsule Collection with Elizabeth Stewart, Hollywood stylist.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, renowned silk brand LILYSILK launched the LILYSILK X Elizabeth Stewart Capsule Collection in partnership with a well-known Hollywood stylist. LILYSILK aims to promote sustainability and inspire individuals to live vibrant lives. This exclusive collection offers a range of luxurious silk products for consumers to enjoy.
